BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. Large and, in isolated cases, very large wet slab avalanches are possible in some places.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes above approximately 1800 m. In the regions exposed to a lot of new snow caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and. In addition the fresh wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are capable of being triggered in some locations. At intermediate altitudes the danger is a little lower. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early. Above approximately 1800 m the danger will increase for a short time to level 4 (high) as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: A lot of snow has fallen over a wide area. In addition sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. Various wind slab layers are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid and thorough wetting of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes also at high altitude.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an additional increase in the danger. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach large size.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are prone to triggering in some cases, especially adjacent to ridgelines on very steep shady slopes. Wind slabs are mostly small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes at high altitude, as well as in all aspects in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and very cloudy skies the snowpack can not consolidate during the night. Up to high altitudes rain will fall on Monday in particular in the south. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly in the early morning. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase quickly in particular on steep sunny slopes. This applies in all aspects at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as on very steep east and south facing slopes in high Alpine regions. During the morning as well, moist snow slides are possible. Wet avalanches can occur easily or triggered naturally. These can also penetrate down to the ground and reach quite a large size especially in the regions with a lot of snow.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach medium size. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good in some case. The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in some cases below approximately 2800 m. On very steep sunny slopes and at the base of rock walls the snowpack will soften quickly.

Older wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on north facing slopes above approximately 2400 m. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations at intermediate and high altitudes. On sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and very cloudy skies the snowpack can not consolidate during the night. Up to high altitudes rain will fall on Monday. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2200 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly in the early morning. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase quickly in particular on steep sunny slopes. This applies in all aspects at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as on very steep east and south facing slopes in high Alpine regions. During the morning as well, moist snow slides are possible. Wet avalanches can occur easily or triggered naturally. These can also penetrate down to the ground and reach quite a large size especially in the regions with a lot of snow.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach medium size. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good in some case. The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in some cases below approximately 2800 m. On very steep sunny slopes and at the base of rock walls the snowpack will soften quickly.

Older wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on north facing slopes above approximately 2400 m. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations at intermediate and high altitudes. On sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and very cloudy skies the snowpack can not consolidate during the night. Up to high altitudes rain will fall on Monday. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2200 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger within the current danger level. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach medium size. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already soften in the late morning. The snowpack will become increasingly wet all the way through. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on steep sunny slopes at high altitude.

Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes at high altitude, as well as in all aspects in high Alpine regions. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations.

On steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and very cloudy skies the snowpack can not consolidate during the night. Up to high altitudes rain will fall on Monday. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger within the current danger level. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach medium size. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already soften in the late morning. The snowpack will become increasingly wet all the way through. This applies in all aspects below approximately 2400 m, as well as on steep sunny slopes at high altitude.

Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes at high altitude, as well as in all aspects in high Alpine regions. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations.

On steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and very cloudy skies the snowpack can not consolidate during the night. Up to high altitudes rain will fall on Monday. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Afternoon: Some new snow above approximately 2300 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On Sunday it will be cloudy. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night.
In the late morning the natural activity of small and medium moist and wet avalanches will only slowly increase.

5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall from the afternoon above approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2400 m. At low altitude and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach quite a large size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
Several medium-sized to large ground avalanches have been released since Tuesday. This applies in particular on very steep northeast, east facing slopes.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of rain up to approximately 1900 m the snowpack can not consolidate. The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Afternoon: Some new snow above approximately 2300 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On Sunday it will be cloudy. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night.
In the late morning the natural activity of small and medium moist and wet avalanches will only slowly increase.

5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall from the afternoon above approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2400 m. At low altitude and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach quite a large size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
Several medium-sized to large ground avalanches have been released since Tuesday. This applies in particular on very steep northeast, east facing slopes.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of rain up to approximately 1900 m the snowpack can not consolidate. The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Afternoon: Wind and new snow above approximately 2300 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On Sunday it will be cloudy. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. In the late morning the natural activity of small and medium moist and wet avalanches will only slowly increase.

15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall from the afternoon above approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2400 m. At low altitude and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach medium size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
In addition sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in particular above the tree line in the evening.
Night: The avalanche danger will increase.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
Several medium-sized to large ground avalanches have been released since Tuesday. This applies in particular on very steep northeast, east facing slopes.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. Already in the morning, Fast increase in avalanche danger level, especially in the areas bordering Piedmont.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Afternoon: Wind and new snow above approximately 2300 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On Sunday it will be cloudy. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. In the late morning the natural activity of small and medium moist and wet avalanches will only slowly increase.

15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall from the afternoon above approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2400 m. At low altitude and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach medium size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
In addition sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in particular above the tree line in the evening.
Night: The avalanche danger will increase.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
Several medium-sized to large ground avalanches have been released since Tuesday. This applies in particular on very steep northeast, east facing slopes.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. Already in the morning, Fast increase in avalanche danger level, especially in the areas bordering Piedmont.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The sleet will give rise from the late morning to moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Up to 2300 m rain will fall from late morning in some regions. This situation will give rise to significant moistening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.

Below approximately 2300 m and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized wet snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In places where the snowfall level is higher the avalanche danger is greater.

The conditions are unfavourable for ski touring, snowshoe hiking as well as off-piste descents, both due to the strong moistening of the snowpack at mid and low altitudes and due to locally poor visibility.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Below approximately 2300 m: The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already be soft in the early morning.
The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A lot of snow will fall on Monday. Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The sleet will give rise from the late morning to moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Up to 2300 m rain will fall from late morning in some regions. This situation will give rise to significant moistening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.

Below approximately 2300 m and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized wet snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In places where the snowfall level is higher the avalanche danger is greater.

The conditions are unfavourable for ski touring, snowshoe hiking as well as off-piste descents, both due to the strong moistening of the snowpack at mid and low altitudes and due to locally poor visibility.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Below approximately 2300 m: The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already be soft in the early morning.
The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A lot of snow will fall on Monday. Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of solar radiation, the natural avalanche activity will gradually increase. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Gliding avalanches can also occur. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. The wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes and generally at elevated altitudes. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. In many places there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Snow cover and its thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude: depending on nighttime and daytime radiation, the snow cover may refreeze in many places or become more moist during the day. At low and intermediate altitudes a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs at elevated altitudes. Moist snow slides and avalanches at low altitude.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
5 to 15 cm of snow will fall from the afternoon above approximately 2200 m.
This situation will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m. At low altitude and on very steep slopes mostly small moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.

In addition mostly small wind slabs will form at intermediate and high altitudes in the afternoon.
High altitudes: On very steep shady slopes the dry avalanches can be triggered in the large-grained old snow and reach medium size in isolated cases. This applies in particular in case of a large load.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 2200 m: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.

High altitudes: Large-grained weak layers exist deep in the old snowpack in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes will become increasingly prone to triggering.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs at elevated altitudes. Moist snow slides and avalanches at low altitude.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
5 to 15 cm of snow will fall from the afternoon above approximately 2200 m.
This situation will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m. At low altitude and on very steep slopes mostly small moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.

In addition mostly small wind slabs will form at intermediate and high altitudes in the afternoon.
High altitudes: On very steep shady slopes the dry avalanches can be triggered in the large-grained old snow and reach medium size in isolated cases. This applies in particular in case of a large load.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 2200 m: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.

High altitudes: Large-grained weak layers exist deep in the old snowpack in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes will become increasingly prone to triggering.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. Moist and wet avalanches are possible during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Isolated whumpfing sounds are a clear indication. In very isolated cases avalanches can also penetrate deep layers and reach quite a large size. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where hard layers are lying on a weakly bonded old snowpack are especially precarious.
The weather will be mild over a wide area. A clear night will be followed in the early morning by quite favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet avalanches will increase later. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be a gradual increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. Medium-sized natural avalanches are possible in particular on very steep slopes. The avalanche prone locations for wet avalanches are to be found in particular on very steep sunny slopes in all altitude zones and in steep rocky terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will soften during the day. These conditions will cause a very rapid weakening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes above the tree line. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible in all altitude zones. More recent wind slabs are clearly recognisable. The wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads, in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach medium size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
These spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects. The various wind slabs have bonded with each other and the old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The rain will give rise in the afternoon to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Up to 2200 m rain will fall in the afternoon in some regions. This situation will give rise to significant moistening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.
In particular below approximately 2300 m and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 2300 m: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A lot of snow will fall on Monday. Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The rain will give rise in the afternoon to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Up to 2200 m rain will fall in the afternoon in some regions. This situation will give rise to significant moistening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.
In particular below approximately 2300 m and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 2300 m: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2300 m. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A lot of snow will fall on Monday. Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Low danger will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase gradually especially on steep northeast and northwest facing slopes. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In the last few days the weather was mild over a wide area. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack.

The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes only a little snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: With the onset of the rainfall, the natural avalanche activity will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase especially on extremely steep shady slopes. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate deep layers and reach medium size in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Low avalanche danger will prevail. As a consequence of mild temperatures very cloudy skies the snowpack can not consolidate during the night. Up to high altitudes rain will fall on Monday in some regions. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.
